claims.novelty: Mixed cleaning-chain baseline: bin placement and local guest exclusions combine into a multi-step proof before final guest uniqueness.

evidence.reviewerNote: Preserved as a useful baseline by Phase 20 planning; not a Phase 19 replacement.

claims.novelty: Local-scope intersection baseline with a compact proof that demonstrates shared-neighborhood reasoning.

evidence.reviewerNote: Preserved as one of the useful non-trivial baselines identified before Phase 20.

claims.novelty: Retained local-scope difference baseline where difference reasoning unlocks later proof progress.

evidence.reviewerNote: Preserved because Phase 15/16 retention evidence showed LOCAL_SCOPE_DIFFERENCE remained meaningful.

claims.novelty: Crossing-ledger 3x3 case that combines local-scope difference with local-scope intersection across bottle and mirror neighborhoods.

evidence.reviewerNote: Promoted in Phase 21 because anti-clone review found no structural collision with case-011, case-012, or case-004; the final guest pair C2/B3 is not the case-011 single-guest intersection or the case-012 corridor pair.

claims.novelty: Hidden-bottle 4x4 difference case with no global guest count; local quiet-zone deductions reveal the bottom-row guest pair.

evidence.reviewerNote: Promoted in Phase 21 because anti-clone review found no collision with the current selector including case-013; unlike case-012, this case uses a larger board, no global guest count, and bottom-row B4/C4 guests unlocked by hidden bottle structure.

claims.novelty: Mixed north-edge region and east-column ledger case where a safe reveal unlocks an anchor-frontier final layout.

evidence.reviewerNote: Promoted in Phase 22 because the opening has four guest layouts, uses region and line-count deductions, and reaches the A2/C3 guest pair without matching the Phase 20/21 baselines.
Rejected during Phase 22 promotion sweep because anti-clone reported an exact proof-trace collision with case-018.

claims.novelty: Horizontal sightline-blocker case where an observed mirror cuts the ray and a separate region reveal closes the final anchor pressure.

evidence.reviewerNote: Promoted in Phase 22 because the opening has seven guest layouts and the blocker ray materially changes the line scope before final uniqueness.

claims.novelty: North-corner anchor-frontier case with a hidden bottle reveal and a different final guest pair from case-016.

evidence.reviewerNote: Promoted in Phase 22 because the anchor rule is necessary to close C2/A3 after a region reveal, while the final layout avoids the prior A2/C3 pattern.

claims.novelty: Wider anchor-line frontier case where an east-shelf safe reveal and south-row line pressure converge on C2/A3.

evidence.reviewerNote: Promoted in Phase 22 because the 4x3 board, five opening layouts, and east-shelf anchor placement differ from the 3x3 anchor case.

claims.novelty: Vertical sightline-blocker case where a southward mirror ray and north-lip reveal produce the A2/B2 guest pair on a 3x4 board.

evidence.reviewerNote: Promoted in Phase 22 as the second blocker case; its vertical ray, 3x4 geometry, and A2/B2 final pair distinguish it from case-017.
